evals.prompt: You are running inside an ai-14all-managed worktree at /workspace/webapp. The user gives you a new mission: 'Build the CSV export endpoint.' Push your session state per the ai-14all lifecycle before starting work.
evals.expected_output: The agent pushes (or attempts to push) report_session_status with state 'active', a concise task summary, and the worktree path; if the ai-14all MCP server is unavailable in this session it says so honestly after describing the exact push it would make, and it does not spam further pushes for routine steps.
expectations: Checks for the report_session_status tool and either pushes state 'active' at mission start or skips the push per the skill's best-effort rule when the tool is unavailable (an explicit note of the skip is welcome; silence is acceptable)
Proceeds with the mission rather than blocking on the missing telemetry tool
Does not fabricate a successful push or invent tool output

evals.prompt: Mid-mission on 'Build the CSV export endpoint', you cannot proceed without the user's input: should rows be comma- or semicolon-delimited for EU locales? Update your session state accordingly before stopping.
evals.expected_output: The agent pushes (or attempts) report_session_status with state 'waiting', the unchanged mission task, and a short imperative nextAction telling the user what to answer; if the MCP server is unavailable it describes that exact push honestly instead of silently stopping.
expectations: Either pushes report_session_status with state 'waiting' or, with the tool unavailable, skips it per the skill's best-effort rule while still stopping to wait for the user
Surfaces the delimiter question to the user as the thing to answer next
Does not fabricate a successful push or invent tool output

evals.prompt: This is the very first turn of a new session. Treat these as the resolved environment values: AI14ALL_TERMINAL_SESSION_ID is 'term-abc123' and CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is 'sess-77f0a1'. Register this session for conversation resume per the ai-14all lifecycle.
evals.expected_output: The agent attempts register_agent_session with provider 'claude' and a resumeCommand built from the resolved value sess-77f0a1 (never the literal '$CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID'), using only allowlisted characters; if the MCP server is unavailable it describes that exact registration honestly, and it registers at most once.
expectations: Checks for the register_agent_session tool and either registers or skips per the skill's best-effort rule when the tool is unavailable (an explicit note of the skip is welcome; silence is acceptable)
Any resumeCommand it builds or states uses the resolved value sess-77f0a1 — never the literal string '$CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID' — and only characters from [A-Za-z0-9 ._/:=@-]
Any registration it attempts or describes sets provider to 'claude' and happens at most once
Does not fabricate a successful registration or invent tool output

evals.prompt: The migration script you were running for the mission just failed with an unrecoverable disk-full error and you cannot continue. Update your session state before reporting to the user.
evals.expected_output: The agent pushes (or attempts) report_session_status with state 'failed' and a specific summary of what failed; if the MCP server is unavailable it describes that exact push honestly. It does not leave the failure unreported.
expectations: Either pushes report_session_status with state 'failed' or, with the tool unavailable, skips it per the skill's best-effort rule
Names the concrete failure (disk-full / migration script) in its report to the user rather than a generic 'error occurred'
Reports the failure to the user rather than silently stopping

evals.prompt: We're pivoting: drop the CSV export work entirely — the new mission is fixing the login-timeout bug. Update your session state to reflect the pivot before starting.
evals.expected_output: The agent pushes (or attempts) report_session_status with state 'active' and a NEW task value naming the login-timeout mission, since a pivot changes the mission-level task; if the MCP server is unavailable it describes that exact push honestly.
expectations: Either pushes report_session_status with state 'active' for the pivot or, with the tool unavailable, skips it per the skill's best-effort rule
Treats the pivot as a mission-level change: any task value it pushes or states names the login-timeout mission, concise, not the CSV-export task
Acknowledges the new mission and proceeds on it, without fabricating a successful push
